preview_text:
The model rates Algeria as the marginal favorite with a 38% win probability, while Austria's chances stand at 30%. This represents a relatively modest gap, placing Algeria ahead but far from dominant in the statistical forecast.

The expected match shape leans competitive rather than one-sided. Draw probability sits at 32%, suggesting a roughly even distribution of outcomes across all three results. The 8-point margin between Algeria's top probability and Austria's reflects a closely balanced contest in which no single outcome substantially outweighs the others.

A clear divergence emerges between the model and market consensus. The sportsbook view favors Austria at 38% to 31% for Algeria—a reversal of roughly 7 percentage points in each direction. Polymarket pricing aligns more closely with the model's lean toward Algeria, though still crediting Austria at 37%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these forecasts; the gap suggests material disagreement on the matchup's underlying dynamics.
